region_groups.name: Pacific Northwest
region_groups.description: Maritime Pacific Northwest and Alaska—cool, wet winters, mild shoulder seasons, and world-class berries, tree fruit, apples, and seafood in coastal states.

regions.name: Washington
regions.description: Washington leads U.S. apples and sweet cherries; western valleys grow hops, wine grapes, and berries while east of the Cascades is drier orchard and grain country.

regions.name: Oregon
regions.description: Oregon’s Willamette Valley and Hood River grow berries, pears, hazelnuts, and specialty seed crops; coastlines favor cool-season greens.

regions.name: Idaho
regions.description: Idaho is famous for russet potatoes, but also produces sugar beets, barley, and summer row crops in irrigated southern valleys.

regions.name: Alaska
regions.description: Alaska’s growing season is intense but short—cool-season greens, brassicas, and greenhouse crops; wild berries and salmon anchor seasonal eating.
